data.title: Traveling
data.description:
It's actually a privilege that my job allow me to travel to different places for work, these are the some of the photo I took in the trip.
There's one reason I have to choose smaller/lighter camera for this purpose, I still need to carry stuff for my job, can't have a whole bag of camera gears.

gallery.title: Random Corner of #Vegas
gallery.description:
Sorry for the slow update on my photostream.
Have been stuck in Vegas for business and also sick for a week, hope the next week will be better~ stay tune~

gallery.title: #Sunset in San Francisco, overlook Bush St. BatterySt. (#Trip #SF)
gallery.description:
I am always fond of the sunset theme in SF, maybe because the latitude, the angle of Sunshine create a very different view from other city, also the architecture of the building in the city play a role in it.
I even think that's the reason that the apple store design their building having a flat shape so that it could reflect the sunshine in a good way.

gallery.title: Siam square night market #travel #photography #bangkok
gallery.description:
It was in my trip last year to Bangkok, walking around the Siam Square night market area before night. I should come again after dark, but forgot to do that, I heard it should be more viral after dark, maybe next time.
Siam Square night market is just around the central world, and near the Siam station in the back alley, it is quite popular place for shopping or having dinner/snacks around. It is also well managed,  compare to some other area in the city.

gallery.title: A grasp of SoCal #Sunset | #Shiny #Trip #SanDiego #RicohGR
gallery.description:
A grasp of winter sunset at SoCal, and yes it is even warm in winter.
It was almost end of the day like now on that day, sunset near the ocean make everything shiny.

Had quite some travel these days, here and there. Will slowly posting some photos from my trips recently, stay tune~

gallery.title: Happy Valentine's Day!!  | #Sunset in #SanDiego #Travel #RicohGR
gallery.description:
Happy Valentine's Day! hope you all have a great one!!
The photo was taken at Del Mar area near San Diego, the sunset there is like this every day.

gallery.title: Yes, I can fly~ 新竹縣市空照 | Overview of Hsinchu, Taiwan (#contax #film #believeinfilm #filmisnotdead #Taiwan #Travel #photography #fly)
gallery.description:
While flying out from TPE airport, as the weather was really nice, I took this shot while we flew over Hsinchu county/city.
The two river from left to right in the picture is Feng-Shan River (鳳山溪) and Tou-Chan River (頭前溪). The Tou-Chan River split Hsinchu county(left side) and city(right side).
Two river merge at Nan-Liao(南竂), it's a fisherman port at the lower right of the picture.

gallery.title: Largo do Senado | 議事廳前地-澳門 (#Travel #Macau #asia)
gallery.description:
Largo do Senado is the square in front of the senate building of Macau. It has become a popular place for shopping and almost everything. There's a fountain which is in the other side of the picture.
Macau is a very interesting place, especially compare with Lisbon. As it was ruled by Portugal for some years, there are buildings, architectures in Portugal style. But not sure that because the Portuguese treat the place as colony and didn't build it the same style/standard, or the time goes by make the city has it own Portugese-Chinese style/culture. The image of two cities are totally different.
Not saying Macau is worse, it is just different, there are some place, we got refined goods from both culture, for example, the Macau style egg tart, I think it is better than the one in Portugal :)

gallery.title: Top of the Haleakalā Mt., Maui HI
gallery.description: The photo was taken on one of the peak of the Haleakalā Mt. Haleakalā Mt located at the east part of the Maui island, the height is about 10,023 feet (3,055 m), it very interesting that a small island like Maui, have such a geographic difference, The view from the top is amazing, and if a good clear day, the ocean and other island could be seen. It's a good place to go, but the trip driving up the mountain is challenging.

gallery.title: 大通公園 | Odori park, Sapporo
gallery.description:
Odori park is a inner city park in the city of Sapporo in Hokkaido, Japan. It located in the center of the city, last about more than 10 blocks, its a good place for citizens to have a place for exercise, and a casual walk in a busy day. The best time to visit the park is in Fall season when the leaves turned yellow and red, and also the winter season when there's Sapporo Snow sculpture festival.
We planed to visit in Fall, but the leaves were not yet turned color, they had Sapporo Fall Food Festival, it was great, but hard to show on picture, will share the picture of the food next time!

gallery.title: The Poster for Sapporo Beer | Sapporo Beer Museum, Sapporo (#Travel #Japan #Sapporo #Beer)
gallery.description: The ad poster for Sapporo Beer over the years, there are some old and famous celebrities of Japan in the posters. Sapporo beer museum is not a big museum, but definitely show all the history for Sapporo beer, and it's a nice place to walk around. Most of all, the tour is free, and you can taste several classic flavor of Sapporo beer inside the museum (not free), and those flavor are not sold other place. reference: www.japan-guide.com/e/e5300.html

gallery.title: Shin-Sapporo area in the day ( #Travel #Japan #Sapporo )
gallery.description:
The building in the front is a typical apartment building in Sapporo area, and is very close to the train station, ideal for young professional or with core family.
Shin Sapporo area is in rural/suburban area of Sapporo, and far from the center of the Sapporo, between the new Chitose airport and main Sapporo station. 
It is developed as a new city, and it is less crowded, but also has less point of interest around here. With express train, it's only couple stop to the Sapporo station. It is a pretty good location for people living here, they will need to have car to commute around though.
*Also see the photo of the night in flickr comment.

gallery.title: Shin-Sapporo area in the night ( #Travel #Japan #Sapporo )
gallery.description:
The building in the front is a typical apartment building in Sapporo area, and is very close to the train station, ideal for young professional or with core family.
Shin Sapporo area is in rural/suburban area of Sapporo, and far from the center of the Sapporo, between the new Chitose airport and main Sapporo station. 
It is developed as a new city, and it is less crowded, but also has less point of interest around here. With express train, it's only couple stop to the Sapporo station. It is a pretty good location for people living here, they will need to have car to commute around though.

gallery.title: Almost done for the day~ | Urban, Sapporo, (#Japan, #Travel)
gallery.description:
Hope you had a good day~
The photo was taken in late afternoon, where people come out between/after work. Hokkaido Japan is not a crowded area compare to place near Tokyo. Sapporo is the biggest city in Hokkaido, and is still very modernized, and could still be crazy in rush hour.

gallery.title: Night view from Moiwa Mountain | Sapporo, Japan
gallery.description:
Moiwa mountain is at the southwest of Japan, it is one of the 3 best night view places in Hokkaido Japan. (Hakodate, Sapporo, Otaru, not sure why there always needed to have 3 of them...).
The observatory is accessible with cable train (Ropeway, they called), with Metro system, one can get the bottom of the hill, and the cable train will take you up to the mountain.
The night view is absolutely gorgeous, and best to come up around sunset time.

gallery.title: メタルスライム | Metal Slime #DQ | Sapporo Beer Factory Museum | #Sapporo, #Japan (#Travel #photography #ishootfilm #filmnotdead)
gallery.description: [update] One of my Google+ friend recognize this as the metal slime ( メタルスライム) in video game Dragon Quest. No wonder it looks familiar. Most people may know Sapporo as a beer brand before knowing its actually a city name. The Sapporo Beer Museum is in out skirt part of Sapporo city, and was factory for manufacturing Sapporo Beer years before. Haven't post for the Sapporo photo for a while, please see the rest of the photo from my trip to Sapporo here, will keep updating.

gallery.title: 札幌-狸小路商店街 Tanukikoji Shopping street | #Sapporo, #Japan (#Travel #photography #ishootfilm #filmnotdead)
gallery.description: continuing photos from my trips~ Sapporo as largest city in Hokkaido area, the Tanukikoji Shopping area is one of the biggest shopping area in the city, however, these years the shops shifted to underground and more close to the main Sapporo station, and left more of the traditional shop in the street. It is a long shopping street last 8 blocks in the city. Reference: www.tanukikoji.or.jp/ www.welcome.city.sapporo.jp/find/shops/tanukikoji_shoppin... See more of the Hokkaido/Sapporo photos here: 2012 Hokkaido trip

gallery.title: Palácio Foz | Lisboa, Portugal
gallery.description: With multiple stops to cross the street, Palácio Foz ( Foz Palace) is another landmark architecture in downtown(Baxia) Lisbon. which it is a huge pink building located west side of Praça dos Restauradores(Restauradores square). It is now served as the national tourism office. They provide some tourism information, and sell some authorized Lisbon souvenir. The palace was once home of the Marquis of Foz. It was built after the 1755 earthquake by the Marques de Castelo Melhor who commissioned the Italian architect Francisco Xavier Fabri to construct the palace in the new styles modeled on the Versailles palace Paris and the Palacio Real de Madrid. In 1889, it is then bought by Marquis of Foz, and renovated in the interior to a more luxurious way. The interior of the palace could be only visit by special permission. Normal tourist have no chance to get inside the palace. However, some folks on the internet did have gone in and taken photo. More photo of the palace on the flickr page. reference: www.lisbonweekendguild.com/lisbon-sights/Praca-Restaurado... www.golisbon.com/sight-seeing/restauradores.html members.virtualtourist.com/m/p/m/1d0f45/

gallery.title: Arch in Praça do Comércio | Lisbon, Portugal
gallery.description: The arch structure surround the main Triumphal Arch in Praça do Comércio (Comércio Square). It's quite some of this kind 7~18 centuries high ceiling architecture in Europe, but very less in US. This one is really nicely built. The building was royal palace and is transformed to museum and restaurant, some of them are still in renovation construction process.

gallery.title: Rua Augusta | Baxia, Lisbon
gallery.description:
This is the downtown area of Lisbon, and was rebuilt around 1755 after the earthquake destroy the place. This street, Rua Augusta lead to Comercio Squre, which is a big square with statue(The statue in the middle of the photo) near by the river.
It is a pedestal only street, there're shops/restaurant in both side of the street. One of the most popular place in Lisbon.

gallery.title: Elevador de Santa Justa | Lisboa, PT
gallery.description:
Santa Justa Lift is also called Carmo Lift which it link lower town/Baxia area and upper town Chiado area. If you purchased the 1-day train ticket, it could be used for the ride, but have to pay more to get to the observatory part.
There's people said that the lift tower is design by the architect who designed the Eiffel Tower in Paris, which is not true. But they do looks assemble in some detail, maybe it's the trend at the time they design the architecture.

gallery.title: Cable train for slope, Lisbon, Portugal
gallery.description: The special cable train for climbing steep slope.

gallery.title: Rua Áurea | Lisboa, Portugal (#travel #photography #lisbon #x100)
gallery.description: This is the downtown area of Lisbon, and was rebuilt around 1755 after the earthquake destroy the place. This area is very near to the river, and the main square, and as there are modern store/shop around here, the city maintain what it was hundred year ago, and pattern on the street is preserved as well. See my other photo of libson here

gallery.title: View of Lisbon  (#Lisbon #travel #photography)
gallery.description:
Lisbon is an interesting city between modern city and historical architecture. where in some part the city preserved what it was hundreds of years ago, and due to the earthquake in 18th century, a big part of city was rebuilt after that, also some part of city is completely modernized.
Also there's an interesting comparison that people made about Lisbon and San Francisco, they share couple similarities. 
1. They both have bays/river.
2, They both have hills in the city.
3. They both have cable car. 
4. They both have bridge cross the bay/river, and it looks alike.
But actually, other than these, they feel totally different.

gallery.title: Cable train in Lisbon, Portugal
gallery.description:
Had a trip to Lisbon couple weeks ago. For Lisbon, they had a renovation of the city which made big road, modern mansions. But the real attracting part is the old, preserved part of the city, where there's narrow street, stone made centuries old building, and with the little yellow cable train stroll through it.
---------------
Chiado, Lisbon, Portugal | Fujifilm X10

gallery.title: Nob Hill Chateau (#Travel #Architecture #SF)
gallery.description: The Nob Hill Chateau, it is a vintage style apartpartment and it is a now a boutique hotel, located at intersection of Powell and Pine in Nob Hill area. The architecture of the building is very nice.

gallery.title: #MerryChristmas | #travel #trip #unionsqure #SF #Macy
gallery.description:
Merry Christmas!!
continue about Christmas scene~
The photo was actual taken 2 years ago, forgot to share this last year, will post couple more.
Union Square in SF is one of the place around having best holiday atmosphere in Christmas time. The classic decoration, with all the high class retail make a great scene.
Is 2 years old photo too old? Hey we are celebrating about something happen two thousand years ago, right? 
Wish you all have happy holidays!!

gallery.title: San Francisco in the Fog
gallery.description:
The fog invaded the city, cover the city like big cap. It's wonderful scene that I believe it happen quite often here.
----------
Shot from the top floor of Fairmont Hotel in Nobhill.

gallery.title: Fort Mason Park |  San Francisco (#SF #Travel #Green #X100)
gallery.description:
It was one fine afternoon at the Fort Mason Park. Just like the weather today.
I like Mason Park which is not far from the city, yet have a good view, and also nice grass land, and good trail for walking or biking.
It is a good place to spend a fine afternoon in weekend~ 
Have a good one!

gallery.title: Fillmore and Beach | #Marina, #SF ( #Travel )
gallery.description: Had chance to walk around the neighborhood from Fortress Mason Park, it is a lovely place, and during the sunset, the houses look even more colorful.

gallery.title: Coffee time~ (Tommy Lee Jones in Japan)
gallery.description: Tommy Lee Jones as Alien agent in Japanese Coffee Commercial. (宇宙人ジョーンズの地球調査シリーズ) Quite interesting and he playing a big part with some Japanese Stars.

gallery.title: Lotte town Star Avenue | Myung-Dong, #Seoul, KR ( #Travel #Korea #x100 )
gallery.description:
Lotte Deportment Store, Seoul, Korea.
Near the Lotte Deportment store, a short alley decorated to be "Star Avenue", where the touch enabled LCD wall interacted with people. It's interesting that people are using it, and when the Star they love shows on screen, they take photo with their cellphone. Why should people take photo off LCD? I don't know, they probably just can't resist their idols shown on a big screen.
There are also signature and hand prints of some famous stars, it's a very small version of the "Korean Hollywood" setup.

gallery.title: Lotte Star Avenue
gallery.description:
Lotte Deportment Store, Seoul, Korea.
The LCD wall in the right side could be interact with touch control. It's interesting that people are using it, and when the Star they love shows on screen, they take photo with their cellphone.

gallery.title: 떡볶이 Korean street food
gallery.description: Suddenly miss the taste of 떡볶이, must because the Korean drama my wife is watching :P

gallery.title: 雨天 | Rainy Day
gallery.description: A Rainy Day in Seoul, Myeong Dong area.

gallery.title: 清溪川 | Cheonggyecheon
gallery.description: Cheonggyecheon is a river goes through the center of Seoul city, and be reconstruct to be a place where citizens can utilize it as a recreational place.

gallery.title: 三清洞 | Samchungdong
gallery.description: Samchungdong is a very special area in Seoul, which consist of old style construction and new open stores, a lot of boutique and coffee shop are around the area.

gallery.title: [iPhone] A view of Seoul, Korea
gallery.description:
A view from my hotel in Seoul, Korea.
Just got back from a 2 week trip. will post more photos! 
AutoStitch + Dynamiclight

data.title: Los Angeles
data.description: Had been lived in Los Angeles area for a while, LA is not a place that one could travel around all the places in few days, even just count the tourist spot, it will take few weeks, since they are distant away. These are the photo I took in LA all these years.

gallery.title: Century City view (#travel #westla)
gallery.description: Just had a trip to Century City. I actually been to the area quite sometime, but not often have chance to be there at night. Century City is one of the many core of LA.

gallery.title: Bradbury Building (#Travel, #DTLA)
gallery.description: In downtown LA, there are several historical building having very special architecture. Well, the number of historical is apparently smaller than Chicago or New York City, but there are some of them are really special, unconventional, Bradbury Building is one of them, with the classic metal frames, and open skylight in the top. And also because of the location, near to Hollywood, there are more chance to see the location in movies. Bradbury Building is the location for several famous movies like Blade Runner, The Artist, 500 days of Summer, etc.

gallery.title: View of the Old L.A. (#travel)
gallery.description:
There's part of LA is consist of historical building in 20s or sometime later/earlier.
I am a big fan for movie "500 days of Summer", and always wanted to visit the spot "Tom's Chair" in Angel's Knoll.
Had a chance to go there couple month ago, and get a view of old LA, and the downtown walking trip, totally worth a trip.

gallery.title: Holiday night at Pasadena
gallery.description: The snow flake projection on the wall changed the atmosphere around Pasadena during holidays.

gallery.title: City of Angels (#LA, #traveling)
gallery.description:
Overlook of West LA, Century City, and downtown LA (in the far end)
Photo taken from Getty Museum.

gallery.title: Abbot Kinney Blvd at evening
gallery.description: Be sure to visit Abbot Kinney Blvd first Friday of the month for the night festival.

gallery.title: Watch baseball game with Dad
gallery.description:
It is a precious moment that father and son spend some time together! Happy belated Fathers day! (8/8 in TW)

gallery.title: Autumn Maple trees
gallery.description:
Found this street at Alhambra, all Maple trees along the street, and most of them are red. Photograph taken with P6 CZJ Biometar 80mm F2.8 adapt with a tilt adapter to Canon 5D Mark II. However, I was going to shift the focus down to the street but failed. The downside is that It is a quite busy street that I lot of car will go through here to get to the main streets.
[IMG_5378]

gallery.title: Reflected
gallery.description:
Take this photo in my way out of the place, at the first I think the black car in front of me is annoying, kind of cut my composition for the school bus. But after find out the reflection of the tree on the car. It actually looks not bad. :-)

Truman: No, we don't have a back up plan. This is it.

I think I will stay home all weekend this week, that will be my plan XD

data.title: Home Coffee Brewing
data.description: A photo set about brewing coffee at home with all kind of tools. Making coffee at home is quite fulfilling task, I  do it almost everyday.

gallery.title: #Coffee time #Pourover #homebrew
gallery.description: Water boiled, cups and pot warmed, ready for the pourover brew.

gallery.title: #Coffee time~ | Happiness comes from Good Coffee~ (@BRUTUS_mag)
gallery.description:
I like magazine from Brutus, even though I couldn't understand it all. (It's a Japanese Magazine, and I could only read a little bit). 
It's a special release cover about coffee and coffee shops in Japan, and US, and some of them in Europe. it's a good companion book for coffee.
Keep brewing, even on holiday~

gallery.title: #Coffee time!
gallery.description: Ready for your coffee break? Brew coffee yourself at home!

gallery.title: #Coffee time (#tonxcoffee #pourover #kalitawave  #hario)
gallery.description: Brewed Tonx coffee today. What's in your cup?

gallery.title: #Coffee time~ | #pourover #homebrew #Kalitawave
gallery.description: Hot water prepared, cups warmed and coffee ground, ready for another pourover, :P

gallery.title: #Coffee time | #Bloom #Pourover #Kalitawave #homebrew
gallery.description: Did you smell the coffee aroma? I am brewing coffee everyday, and for some reason, I never get bored taking these pictures.

gallery.title: #Coffee time! | (#pourover #Kalitawave #homebrew #photography)
gallery.description:
Brewed with Kalitawave today~ what's in your cup today?
[Detail]
- Coffee: Tonx Kagumo-Ini, Nyeri, Kenya, 18g
- Grinder setting: Fuji R220, 4.5
- Water/ temp.: 250cc / 85c
- Kettle: Tsuki Usagi Slim pot 0.7L
- Brewer: Kalita wave 185, Stainless/ Kalita wave 185 filter
- Brew time: bloom for 45 sec, complete in 3:30

gallery.title: #Coffee time | Keep Claim and #pourover #TGIF
gallery.description:
I think I just never get bored of brewing coffee, looking forward everyday for doing another brew. Not sure I am expecting a good cup or to hand on the brewing, maybe both :)
By the way, have a good weekend!!

gallery.title: #Coffee time~ | #Kalitawave #Pourover
gallery.description:
Received my new beans today, and did the Kalitawave pour-over.
It smells good and tastes great!

gallery.title: #Coffee time~ | #Hario woodneck cloth filter brew (#TGIF #Pourover)
gallery.description: TGIF, Did the brew with Hario Woodneck with cloth filter~ What's in your cup today? Have a great weekend!! [Brew detail] coffee: Tonxcoffee XInacla, Marcala, Honduras 15g, grind: 5.0 with Fuji R220 water 230 c.c. method: Pour over with Hario woodneck, cloth filter.

gallery.title: Magic Crystal Pot, tell me what will the... (#coffee #hario #woodneck #photography)
gallery.description:
Fortune telling with my coffee pot, not really working, didn't figure out which stock to buy~
Gipsy people could do that with the crystal ball, there gotta be a way...
Anyway, forget about the fortune, the coffee was very nice.
By the way, you can see the crystal ball if you turn the photo upside down.

gallery.title: #Coffee time! | #Hario Woodneck Cloth filter brew
gallery.description:
I got the Hario Woodneck for a while, it has the same retro look that Chemex has, and most differently, it come with a cloth filter. With the cloth filter, it save some paper, but most importantly, a different taste, the cloth some how made the taste more clear and smooth.
and having the coffee bloom in your hand is a good presentation as well.

gallery.title: Coffee time~ | Pre-infusion
gallery.description:
Had my brew with Hario V60. I ran out of the filter a week ago, and got stock in the holiday with good a deal. First day after the holiday, first brew of the week, and used the first filter in the new bag, done my first brew step right -- the pre-infusion. 
Hope you all got the first thing right start off the new week!

gallery.title: Coffee time~ (Hario v60)
gallery.description: My Hario v60 set, well, I never do a side by side taste compare with my old Melitta style dripper. Maybe I just like the way it looks.

gallery.title: Coffee time - The big bloom theory
Coffee time~
gallery.description: My box of Tonx coffee and Chemex~ you can get yours here :)

gallery.title: #Coffee time~ | #Siphon coffee brewing
gallery.description:
Did you have coffee today?
I got a beam heater recently for the Siphon brewing.
It provide better heat control compare to the gas stove, it's not dramatically different in term of usability, but you can't beat the fantastic visual effect!

gallery.title: #Coffee time~ | #Siphon brew ( #Kono #photography )
gallery.description:
Siphon brew with beam heater and Kono siphon brewer at home :)

[Brew information]
Kono SKD2 Siphon Brewer (ceramic with cloth filter)
Akira Beam Heater
Coffee: Four Barrel Kenya Tegu 20g
Water 250cc
Brew time 60 sec.

[Camrea information]
Canon 5D Mk2
Contax Carl Zeiss Macro Planar 60/F2.8 Compact T*
(Manual adapted)

gallery.title: Brewing #siphon #coffee at home
gallery.description:
Brewing siphon coffee at home today. Got my Kono siphon brewer couple mouth ago shipped from Asia. I have used siphon brewer couple time many years ago. But didn't have one since moved to US.
The Kono brewer come with the cloth and ceramic filter, and the one I have is a 2 cup base and top.
I like the taste very much, as most siphon brewing, the taste feel full-bodied, and brewing process is fun (but have to be careful...)

gallery.title: Perfect temperature? (#coffee #pourover #homebrew #temperature)
gallery.description:
There's a theory that coffee will be brewed perfectly at 85 degree Celsius (185 F)
I guess it's hard to achieve a perfect number, that there are so many variable to concern about. Well, I just like the idea that having a thermometer and knowing the number, got the thermometer from my trip to Macau, introduce by one of the barista. It works pretty nice, it's digital, but it detects very fast.

gallery.title: Coffee time~
gallery.description: Just tasted a new bag of bean today :)

gallery.title: Coffee time~ (Cold Brew Coffee)
gallery.description: As the weather getting hotter these days, it's a nice indulgence to have a cup of iced/cold coffee in the day. We brought back a pack of cold brew coffee in my trip to Okinawa. (Yes, Okinawa, a place seems nothing to do with coffee.) Please see them below.

gallery.title: #Coffee time~ | @fourbarrel #homebrew #pourover #coffeegrinder
gallery.description: Got bean from Four Barrel up in SF, and brew at home.

gallery.title: Grinder dial in - Batazta Vario | #coffee #homebarista #espresso #grinder
gallery.description:
A good grinder makes a big difference. I use Vario for quite some time.
It's a good reliable grinder, however once a while a calibration is needed.
Especially when change from different brewing method and coffee beans.
For espresso, a good shot with fresh bean time about 26 sec. will be the best.
Well, at least that's what I heard, please share some tip I'd you have any special parameter for dial in a grinder.

gallery.title: #Coffee time~ #espresso #homebarista
gallery.description:
Trying to achieve a 27 sec perfect shot~
What do you have for coffee today?

Had coffee with Areopress with the Disc fine from Able brewing today~
Although it took some effort to press out the coffee (maybe I set the grinder too fine), the coffee is great!

gallery.title: Coffee time~ (aeropress!)
gallery.description:
A bit late in the afternoon, but not too late for coffee :P
Had a cup with Aeropress today, how about you?

gallery.title: Traveling with Coffee? Why not~ (#travel #coffee)
gallery.description: I brew my fresh coffee almost every morning, I wasn't able to do that while traveling, not until I got the Porlex grinder. The Porlex mini is very portable, and you could also bring beans and any brewing device with you. I did Aeropress in my last trip. Using hot water from hotel coffee machine, and bring or buy fresh bean on road, ground right before brewing.

gallery.title: Coffee time~
gallery.description: Can't wait to receive my bean from tonx.org :) They let you try for free bag of bean. You can decide whether you want to subscribe after you tried, no pressure but only free beans :P

gallery.title: Coffee time
gallery.description: Time to have coffee :)

gallery.title: Coffee Time~ | Nespresso, Kona
gallery.description:
Nespresso has this new limited reserved flavor using Hawaii Kona beans. I got some and tried, it is really nice especially drink as espresso, they definitely did preserve the signature of Kona beans.
"a fruity bouquet revealing delicate hint of nuttiness" according the description from the official website.
The price is a little high, $2 per capsule. It may not be a good option for making cappuccino as the intensity is low (5).
TGIF, hope you all have a good Friday, and weekend!

gallery.title: #Coffee time~ | brewing #Nespresso #Crema
gallery.description:
For Nespresso, there's not much work to do for making a cup a coffee. Add water, put in capsule, and press a button, and you are done~ Also the good thing is that the capsule could preserve for quite some time, it is really convenience.
Of course, the draw back include the high price (capsule price, capsule shipping price/cost, machine price. machine maintenance cost), and limited flavor, and no choice of coffee origin.
I do it once in a while that I have limited time, or out of stock of bean, and in the case for today -- construction in the kitchen.

gallery.title: Coffee time (Nespresso)
gallery.description: Once in a while, we will have Nespresso. It's good choice for those who have no time to prepare and clean, and no need for trained skill, just that the capsule cost a little more.

gallery.title: DyDo Cafe Au Lait
gallery.description: Coffee time again! tried another Japanese coffee drink, with the similar bottle package, they have milk tea, coffee milk, and this one cafe au lait. I like it, especially the white bottle look :)

gallery.title: Japanese Can Coffee
gallery.description:
In LA, all kind of product from are imported and sold here. As coffee is one my favorite drink, sometimes I try these Japanese can coffee. However, might because of the import, they are quite expensive, 2$~4$ for a can, and which they are smaller than the normal coke can. It is the same size of the Starbuck double shot can, but they are not espresso, they are just normal brewed/can coffee. I found out that I like the can more than the drink XD. 
(Canon EOS 5D Mark II | Distagon 25mm F2.8 | Storbist: Sun light in the back, A4 paper as reflective in the front)
